title: Avoid non-existent operators
description: Using non-existent operators like '=+' instead of '+=' indicates a syntax error or typo that can lead to unexpected behavior. These mistakes can cause logic errors and make code behave differently than intended.
recommendation: Use correct operator syntax and fix any typos in operator usage.

noncompliant.description: Using incorrect operator syntax like =+, =*, =! instead of +=, *=, != leads to unexpected behavior.

compliant.description: Using correct operator syntax ensures the code behaves as intended and is easier to understand.

checks.message: Use correct operator syntax - += not =+, != not =!
